raw_consumer_statement: I registered for an exam with XXXX for {$350.00} in XX/XX/year>, with a test date of XX/XX/XXXX. 2024. On XX/XX/year>, I needed to reschedule my test for XX/XX/year>, and was charged a {$40.00} change fee. However, I was charged the {$40.00} by XXXX, but they never scheduled the new test date. I contacted the company on XX/XX/year>, because I did not see the latest test while reviewing my account. XXXX reported that my payment never went through, so the new test date was not scheduled. However, XXXX advised they would refund the {$40.00} change fee, and the refund was received on XX/XX/year>, and confirmed by American Express. However, XXXX says I need to pay {$350.00} again to take my original exam because they did not receive the {$40.00} change fee, and my original test date remained. Please help me understand how this makes any sense. How can you refund the money you never received? Since American Express knows about all the transactions, why dont they refund my {$350.00}? XXXX will not allow me to take my exam without paying another {$350.00} or refund my money.
